Get in touch
003 — Science

Active research.
Not slide decks.

Our research areas sit at the intersection of genomics, machine learning, and clinical translation. We publish, we build, and we share the tools.

01
Spatial Genomics

Spatial Multiome Architecture

Mapping gene expression and chromatin accessibility in tissue space using Visium HD, MERSCOPE, and Xenium. We reconstruct tumor microenvironment architecture and cell-cell interaction networks at submicron resolution — going beyond counts to spatial context.

Visium HD MERSCOPE Xenium SpatialDE
02
Computational Drug Design

Protein Language Models for Drug Discovery

Fine-tuning ESM-3 and ProtTrans on target-specific datasets for zero-shot binding affinity prediction, peptide drug design, and antibody CDR loop optimization. Building task-specific protein LLMs that generalize across druggable target families.

ESM-3 ProtTrans Antibody Design Peptide ML
03
Epigenomics

Epigenomic Reprogramming Maps

CUT&RUN, CUT&TAG, and scATAC profiling to map transcription factor binding dynamics, super-enhancer activation, and chromatin remodeling across oncogenic transformation and therapeutic response. Linking regulatory grammar to disease phenotype.

CUT&RUN scATAC-seq Super-Enhancers TF Motifs
04
Translational Oncology

Pan-Cancer Single-Cell Atlas

A continuously updated reference atlas integrating curated public and proprietary single-cell datasets across tumor types. Used for automated cell type annotation, rare cell population discovery, and survival-associated gene module identification.

Cell Atlas TCGA Survival Analysis scGPT
05
Generative Biologics

De Novo Protein & Antibody Synthesis

Leveraging diffusion models and RFdiffusion to design high-affinity binders from scratch. We integrate structural biology with generative AI to bypass traditional screening, accelerating the development of therapeutic proteins with programmable specificity.

RFdiffusion ProteinMPNN De Novo Design Structure-to-Function
06
Precision Pharmacogenomics

Predictive Safety & ADR Modeling

Integrating genomic variants with large-scale pharmacovigilance data to predict Adverse Drug Reactions (ADRs). We build deep learning architectures that map the intersection of genetic markers and chemical toxicity to enable truly personalized medicine.

ADR Prediction GWAS Integration Drug-Target Interaction Safety Intelligence
07
Biomolecular Structure

End-to-End Co-Complex Structure Prediction

Running Boltz-2 and AlphaFold3 pipelines for joint prediction of protein-ligand, protein-nucleic acid, and antibody-antigen co-complexes at scale. Confidence-calibrated structural ensembles feed directly into our docking and free-energy perturbation workflows.

Boltz-2 AlphaFold3 Co-Complex Prediction Free-Energy Perturbation
08
Multimodal Foundation Models

Cross-Modal Genomic Representation Learning

Pretraining and fine-tuning large biological foundation models — scFoundation, Geneformer, and in-house architectures — on paired transcriptomic, proteomic, and imaging datasets. Unified representations that generalize across cell types, tissues, and perturbation screens.

scFoundation Geneformer Perturbation Biology Multi-Modal Learning
09
Long-Read Genomics

Full-Length Variant & Isoform Resolution

PacBio Revio and Oxford Nanopore R10 workflows for phased structural variant calling, full-length transcript isoform quantification, and direct base modification detection. Closing the gaps left by short-read sequencing in complex genomic regions and repeat elements.

PacBio Revio ONT R10 Structural Variants Direct RNA Sequencing

Doing interesting
work? Let's make it boring.

We're always open to research collaborations, data partnerships, and advisory conversations.

Boring Science LLC builds the infrastructure that turns biological noise into structured insight.
